title: Casearia povedae
taxonomy: Casearia povedae is classified within the family Salicaceae and the order Malpighiales. This plant is a member of the genus Casearia, which consists of approximately 150 species of flowering plants. The genus Casearia is further classified within the family Salicaceae, which includes willows, poplars, and other related species. Casearia povedae is native to Central and South America, where it grows in humid and subtropical regions. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Malpighiales order.
